instruction:
Let $V, W$ be complex vector spaces of dimensions $m \geq n \geq 2$, respectively. Let ${\mathbb{P}\text{Hom}(V, W) \cong \mathbb{P}^{mn-1} }$be the projective space of nonzero linear maps $\phi: V \to W$modulo scalars. Further, let $\Phi \subset \mathbb{P}\text{Hom}(V, W)$ be the subset of those linear maps $\phi$which do not have full rank $n$ . $\Phi$is an irreducible subvariety of $\mathbb{P}^{mn-1}$ .What is its dimensionality?

Options:
A. $$
mn-n^2-1
$$
B. $$
m^2-nm+m-1
$$
C. $$
mn-m^2+n-2
$$
D. $$
m^2-n^2-2
$$
E. $$
mn-m+n-3
$$
F. $$
mn-n+m-4
$$
G. $$
mn-n-3m+1
$$
H. $$
m^2-nm+n+3
$$
I. $$
mn-m+n-2
$$
J. $$
m^2-n+2
$$
